category_title: Adolescent with Down's syndrome exhibiting verbal outbursts to escape demands or gain tangibles
student_description: Dennis is a 15-year-old student with Down's syndrome and an intellectual disability who communicates via short utterances and gestures. He frequently engages in verbal outbursts ('No', 'Stop it') to escape rapid instructional demands or to gain access to preferred food items when he is hungry. His behavior is highly susceptible to observational reactivity, meaning his true behavioral patterns only emerge in his natural environment rather than in artificial or analogue settings.

criteria.rationale: Dennis engages in verbal outbursts to escape high-frequency instructional demands, such as being prompted every 10 seconds.
criteria.check: The tutor's immediate response avoids rapid, repetitive prompting and instead adjusts the pacing, breaks down the task, or alters the demand presentation to reduce the drive to escape.

criteria.rationale: Placing preferred food items in sight but out of reach while asking Dennis to wait triggers severe outbursts, especially when he is in a hunger state.
criteria.check: The tutor avoids placing preferred items in sight but out of reach, either by keeping the item out of sight until it is time to eat or by managing access in a way that does not provoke tangible-seeking outbursts.
